name: -e
--eval
options.description: Evaluates the given command

name: -h
--help
options.description: Prints this message and exits

name: -r
options.description: Requires the given files/patterns

name: -S
options.description: Finds and executes the given script in $PATH

name: -pr
options.description: Requires the given files/patterns in parallel

name: -pa
options.description: Prepends the given path to Erlang code path

name: -pz
options.description: Appends the given path to Erlang code path

name: -v
--version
options.description: Prints Elixir version and exits

name: --app
options.description: Starts the given app and its dependencies

name: --erl
options.description: Switches to be passed down to Erlang

name: --logger-otp-reports
options.description: Enables or disables OTP reporting

name: --logger-sasl-reports
options.description: Enables or disables SASL reporting

name: --no-halt
options.description: Does not halt the Erlang VM after execution

name: --werl
options.description: Uses Erlang's Windows shell GUI (Windows only)

name: --cookie
options.description: Sets a cookie for this distributed node

name: --hidden
options.description: Makes a hidden node

name: --name
options.description: Makes and assigns a name to the distributed node

name: --rpc-eval
options.description: Evaluates the given command on the given remote node

name: --sname
options.description: Makes and assigns a short name to the distributed node

name: --boot
options.description: Uses the given FILE.boot to start the system

name: --boot-var
options.description: Makes $VAR available as VALUE to FILE.boot

name: --erl-config
options.description: Loads configuration in FILE.config written in Erlang

name: --pipe-to
options.description: Starts the Erlang VM as a named PIPEDIR and LOGDIR

name: --vm-args
options.description: Passes the contents in file as arguments to the VM
